result.markdown:
--- title: "Data Analysis: Automated Data Pipeline" description: "Scraping, cleaning, and visualizing data." --- ## The Problem We needed a reliable way to track the list of largest companies in the US by revenue, but the data was on another 3rd party website. ## Web Scraping & Cleaning I built an automated pipeline using Python, BeautifulSoup, and Pandas to extract and structure the data. ::: {#d4a64fea .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd from bs4 import BeautifulSoup import requests url = 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_largest_companies_in_the_United_States_by_revenue' page = requests.get(url, headers = {'User-Agent': 'CustomBot (xyz@gmail.com)'}) soup = BeautifulSoup(page.text, 'html') ## print(soup) soup.find_all('table')[0] soup.find('table', class_ = 'wikitable sortable') table = soup.find_all('table')[0] ## print(table) company_titles = table.find_all('th') company_titles company_table_titles = [title.text.strip() for title in company_titles] ## print(company_table_titles) df = pd.DataFrame(columns = company_table_titles) df column_data = table.find_all('tr') for row in column_data[1:]: row_data = row.find_all('td') individual_row_data = [data.text.strip() for data in row_data] length = len(df) df.loc[length] = individual_row_data df ## df.to_csv(r'/Users/nav/WebScraping for Output\Companies.csv', index = False) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
Rank Name Industry Revenue (USD millions) Revenue growth Employees Headquarters
